text:
There once was a young girl who had a peculiar pet hamster named Hopper. She named him that because no one else would play with him when he'd hop around the house. Hopper was a cheerful little critter, with big, wise eyes and fur the color of freshly roasted carrots.

One day, while playing outside, she heard a strange noise. Hopper twitched his nose and twitched his tail. They listened. It was the distant buzz of something sweet smelling. After a moment, Hopper darted into his ball pit.

After a few seconds, he emerged, whining. There was a bright red pill on top of a plastic stick!

She picked it up, and it tasted sweet. She tossed it to Hopper, who flew over with joy. Day after day, they found more—apparently, a little red worm lived beneath the leaves. They dubbed it "Pillberry."

One evening, her family arrived from out-of-state to visit. They
